commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a QA Lab channel behavior scenario module, exports it from the QA runtime API, allowlists it in `qaImport`, and adds contract tests.

PR surface: Source +314, Tests +298. Total +612 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The PR's CI run reproduces the merge blocker with `pnpm check:architecture`, where the Madge check reports the new `channel-behavior-scenario.ts -> runtime-api.ts` cycle.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can address the import-cycle blocker without choosing a new product direction.

target.title: test(qa): route DM baseline through channel scenario driver
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The QA real-E2E migration plan needs channel scenarios to be routed through a reusable behavior contract instead of hand-wiring low-level `state.addInboundMessage` / `waitForOutboundMessage` calls in every YAML file. Without that shared path, live, Crabline, and qa-channel variants can drift and weak scenarios can look covered while skipping thread/reply/no-reply semantics.

## Why This Change Was Made

Adds the QA Lab channel behavior scenario module with typed scenario definitions, expected reply/no-reply steps, restart/thread/reply requirement summaries, target/message helpers, and a `ChannelScenarioDriver` interface.

This now also wires one concrete existing scenario through that path: `qa/scenarios/channels/dm-chat-baseline.yaml` defines a channel behavior scenario and runs it through the QA-flow driver adapter instead of directly manipulating the QA bus.

The runner deliberately fails fast for reply-targeting steps until those semantics are implemented, and created-thread reply expectations are pinned to the dynamic thread id to avoid false-positive threaded evidence.

AI-assisted: yes.

## User Impact

No end-user runtime behavior change. QA authors now have a shared contract and a working example for routing channel behavior through a pluggable driver, with `dm-chat-baseline` proving the path against the existing qa-channel suite.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/channel-behavior-scenario.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/scenario-flow-runner.test.ts --reporter=verbose` pass, 19 tests.
- `pnpm openclaw qa suite --provider-mode mock-openai --scenario dm-chat-baseline --output-dir .artifacts/qa-e2e/dm-channel-driver-smoke-rerun` pass, 1 scenario passed / 0 failed.
- `pnpm check:test-types` pass.
- `pnpm deadcode:unused-files` pass.
- `pnpm deadcode:dependencies` pass.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/qa-lab/src/channel-behavior-scenario.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/channel-behavior-scenario.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/runtime-api.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/scenario-flow-runner.test.ts qa/scenarios/channels/dm-chat-baseline.yaml` pass.
- `git diff --check` pass.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` clean after fixing scoped findings around reply-targeting fail-fast behavior, bound thread creation, and dynamic thread reply expectations.
target.author: romneyda

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
